seo.title: RAG Retrieval Quality Optimization in DSPy Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix rag retrieval quality optimization in DSPy agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In DSPy, RAG Retrieval Quality Optimization issues manifest when retrieval modules fail to fetch relevant context, leading to degraded LM performance despite signature optimization. DSPy's optimizers like BootstrapFewShot can overfit prompts to noisy retrieved contexts, producing plausible but inaccurate responses. This is exacerbated by module composition where ChainOfThought or Retrieve modules propagate low-quality retrievals upstream, violating assertions at inference.

howItManifests.scenario: BootstrapFewShot optimizer tunes RAGChain on dev set with irrelevant retrieved chunks
howItManifests.symptom: High train accuracy but dev set responses cite wrong documents with confident tone
howItManifests.impact: Production hallucinations increase 3x as model trusts bad context

howItManifests.scenario: Retrieve module returns top-k passages with low semantic similarity to query
howItManifests.symptom: ChainOfThought module generates elaborate reasoning chains citing irrelevant facts

howItManifests.scenario: Multi-hop RAG composes Retrieve → GenerateAnswer → Retrieve
howItManifests.symptom: Second retrieval drifts due to first generation's factual errors
howItManifests.impact: Final answer accuracy drops from 85% to 32% across composition

howItManifests.scenario: ColBERTv2 retriever optimized but returns passages with keyword overlap only
howItManifests.impact: Unexpected output distribution: 60% confident wrong vs 20% uncertain correct

detection.name: Retrieval Relevance Score Monitor
detection.description: Wrap Retrieve forward() and compute cosine similarity between query embedding and top-k passages using DSPy's ColBERTv2

detection.name: RAG Assertion Chain Validator
detection.description: Add DSPy assertions to verify retrieved context contains answer keywords and spans
detection.code:
class RAGWithAssertions(dspy.Module):
    def __init__(self):
        self.retrieve = dspy.Retrieve(k=5)
        self.generate = dspy.ChainOfThought('query, context -> answer')
    
    def forward(self, query):
        context = self.retrieve(query)
        prediction = self.generate(query=query, context=context.passages)
        
        # Assert context relevance
        context_words = ' '.lower().split()
        answer_words = prediction.answer.lower().split()
        overlap = len(set(answer_words) & set(context_words)) / len(answer_words)
        
        assert overlap > 0.3, f'Low context overlap: {overlap:.2f}'
        
        return prediction

detection.name: Optimizer Drift Detector
detection.description: Compare pre/post-optimization retrieval distributions using KL divergence
detection.code:
import numpy as np
from scipy.stats import entropy

class OptimizationDriftDetector:
    def __init__(self, baseline_retriever):
        self.baseline = baseline_retriever
        
    def detect_drift(self, optimized_retriever, test_queries):
        baseline_scores = []
        opt_scores = []
        
        for query in test_queries[:100]:
            base_ctx = self.baseline(query).passages
            opt_ctx = optimized_retriever(query).passages
            
            baseline_scores.append(np.mean([len(p.split()) for p in base_ctx]))
            opt_scores.append(np.mean([len(p.split()) for p in opt_ctx]))
        
        kl_div = entropy(np.histogram(baseline_scores, bins=10)[0], 
                        np.histogram(opt_scores, bins=10)[0])
        
        if kl_div > 0.5:
            print(f'OPTIMIZER DRIFT DETECTED: KL={kl_div:.3f}')
        
        return kl_div

fixes.name: Hybrid Dense+Sparse Retrieval
fixes.description: Combine ColBERTv2 dense retrieval with BM25 sparse for diverse, relevant passages

fixes.name: Retrieval Constrained Optimization
fixes.description: BootstrapFewShot with retrieval quality metric in metric fn
fixes.code:
def rag_metric_with_retrieval(example, pred, trace=None):
    # Standard answer accuracy
    acc = dspy.evaluate.answer_exact_match(example, pred)
    
    # Retrieval quality (context must contain gold answer keywords)
    gold_words = set(example.answer.lower().split())
    ctx_words = set(' '.join(trace.retrieval.context).lower().split())
    retrieval_rel = len(gold_words & ctx_words) / len(gold_words)
    
    return acc * 0.7 + retrieval_rel * 0.3

# Use in optimizer
teleprompter = BootstrapFewShot(metric=rag_metric_with_retrieval)
optimized_rag = teleprompter.compile(rag_module, trainset=trainset)

fixes.name: Self-Reflective RAG with Re-retrieval
fixes.description: Add confidence-based re-retrieval when initial retrieval scores low
fixes.code:
class ReflectiveRAG(dspy.Module):
    def __init__(self):
        self.retrieve = dspy.Retrieve(k=5)
        self.generate = dspy.ChainOfThought('query, context -> answer, confidence')
        self.rerank = ColBERTv2()
        
    def forward(self, query):
        context = self.retrieve(query)
        pred = self.generate(query=query, context=context.passages)
        
        if pred.confidence < 0.7:
            print('Low confidence, re-retrieving...')
            # Use answer snippet to refine query
            refined_query = f'{query} {pred.answer[:50]}'
            context = self.rerank(refined_query, k=5)
            pred = self.generate(query=refined_query, context=context.passages)
        
        return pred

vexAutomation.heading: Automating RAG Retrieval Quality Optimization Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex wraps DSPy module forward() calls to automatically capture retrieval traces, compute relevance metrics, and flag quality violations without changing agent code. It detects the four common failure modes by analyzing input/output distributions and assertion violations in real-time during optimization and inference. Vex's DSPy integration runs continuously across teleprompter.compile() and production predict() calls, surfacing issues like overfitting retrieval prompts or composition drift before they impact accuracy.

Remediation workflows trigger hybrid retrieval upgrades, constrained optimization, or self-reflective reranking when violations exceed thresholds. Vex maintains a historical baseline of healthy retrieval distributions, alerting when KL divergence indicates optimizer drift. This ensures RAG quality remains stable across DSPy program evolution.
vexAutomation.code:
import vex
import dspy

# 1. Decorate your DSPy RAG module
@vex.verify(
    assertions={
        'retrieval_relevance': 'avg_cosine_sim(query_emb, top5_passages) > 0.7',
        'no_drift': 'kl_div(retrieval_scores, baseline_scores) < 0.5',
        'context_overlap': 'answer_keywords_in_context > 0.3'
    },
    remediation='upgrade_to_hybrid_retrieval'
)
class ProductionRAG(dspy.Module):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.retrieve = dspy.Retrieve(k=5)
        self.generate = dspy.ChainOfThought('query, context -> answer')
    
    def forward(self, query):
        ctx = self.retrieve(query)
        pred = self.generate(query=query, context=ctx.passages)
        return pred

# 2. Vex auto-instruments optimizer too
teleprompter = dspy.BootstrapFewShot()
optimized_rag = teleprompter.compile(ProductionRAG(), trainset=trainset)

# Vex dashboard shows: 23% retrieval violations, auto-applied hybrid fix

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex (`pip install vex-sdk`) and catch RAG quality issues before they tank your DSPy agent's production accuracy.
